commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Google Gemini transport to omit `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig` when `options.cachedContent` is set, and updates direct transport tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source and tests show the conflicting request shape, and the PR still leaves the managed `cacheRetention` wrapper path source-reproducible because `cachedContent` is patched after the builder runs.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can complete the missing managed prompt-cache half of the fix on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(google): omit request config with cached content
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #84919.
- Omits per-request `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig` from direct Gemini GenerateContent requests when `cachedContent` is set.
- Keeps generation settings such as `generationConfig` intact and adds regression coverage for the cached-content payload shape.

## Root Cause
OpenClaw's Google transport added `cachedContent` to the GenerateContent payload, then independently serialized the current system prompt and tool configuration. Google requires `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig` to be part of the cached content resource when a request uses `cachedContent`; sending them again on GenerateContent can return HTTP 400.

## Why This Is Safe
The change is scoped to direct Gemini request construction. It only changes payloads with a non-empty `options.cachedContent`; uncached Gemini requests still send system prompts and tools exactly as before. Cached-content requests still include `contents` and `generationConfig`, so temperature, max tokens, and thinking settings continue to apply.

Security and runtime controls are unchanged: guarded model fetch, auth resolution, request URL/header construction, tool execution policy, Slack/channel policy, and cache resource creation/refresh behavior are not modified. This is runtime-enforced payload construction, not prompt-text policy.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Gemini GenerateContent requests that use `cachedContent` no longer send per-request `systemInstruction`, `tools`, or `toolConfig`. Google rejects that combination with:

```text
CachedContent can not be used with GenerateContent request setting system_instruction, tools or tool_config.
```

Real environment tested:
- Host: local macOS checkout.
- OpenClaw wrapper under test: `OpenClaw 2026.5.20 (bde07dd)`.
- Code under test: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`.
- Model/provider: `google/gemini-2.5-flash` via the real Google Gemini API.
- Auth source: trusted OpenClaw runtime dotenv resolved a Gemini API key; secret value was not logged.
- Slack transport attempt: two real Slack bot tokens and one app-level token from local `.env`, with both bots installed in the same workspace/channel; IDs redacted below.

Exact live Gemini steps after this patch:
1. Created a real Gemini `cachedContents` resource with a cached prefix above Gemini's 1024-token cache minimum.
2. Included `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig` at cache creation time, matching Google cached-content semantics.
3. Sent a GenerateContent request through OpenClaw's patched Google transport with `cachedContent` set, while the OpenClaw context still had a system prompt and tool definition.
4. Captured only the sanitized request shape through the transport `onPayload` hook.
5. Verified Google accepted the request, returned a model response, and reported `cachedContentTokenCount` mapped to `cacheRead`.
6. Deleted the Gemini `cachedContents` resource after the run.

Redacted live Gemini log excerpt:

```text
create cachedContents request shape:
  model: models/gemini-2.5-flash
  contents: present, large stable cached prefix
  systemInstruction: present at cache creation
  tools: present at cache creation
  toolConfig: present at cache creation
  ttl: 300s

create cachedContents response:
  name prefix: cachedContents
  usageMetadata.totalTokenCount: 6990

OpenClaw GenerateContent payload shape after patch:
  cachedContent: present
  systemInstruction key present: false
  tools key present: false
  toolConfig key present: false
  generationConfig key present: true

GenerateContent result:
  ok: true
  stopReason: stop
  contentTypes: text
  response text marker: OPENCLAW_GEMINI_CACHE_PROOF_30IU1K
  usage.input: 25
  usage.output: 121
  usage.cacheRead: 6990
  usage.cacheWrite: 0
  usage.totalTokens: 7136

cleanup:
  deletedCachedContent: true
```

Observed result after fix: the real Gemini API accepted the cached-content GenerateContent request. The captured OpenClaw payload had `cachedContent` present and omitted all three conflicting fields: `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig`. The provider response reported `cacheRead=6990`, proving the request used the cached-content path instead of falling back to an uncached request.

Slack two-bot proof attempt:

```text
SLACK_CHANNEL_ID present: true
Slack app token present: true
slack_token1: auth.ok=true team=<redacted> user=<redacted> bot=<redacted>
slack_token1: channel.ok=true isMember=true isChannel=true
slack_token3: auth.ok=true team=<redacted> user=<redacted> bot=<redacted>
slack_token3: channel.ok=true isMember=true isChannel=true
```

Command shape:

```text
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID=<redacted channel id>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted xoxb for driver bot>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_SUT_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted xoxb for SUT bot>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_SUT_APP_TOKEN=<redacted xapp token>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_CAPTURE_CONTENT=1
OPENCLAW_QA_REDACT_PUBLIC_METADATA=0
pnpm openclaw qa slack \
  --credential-source env \
  --scenario slack-canary \
  --provider-mode live-frontier \
  --model google/gemini-2.5-flash \
  --alt-model google/gemini-2.5-flash \
  --output-dir .artifacts/slack-gemini-live-proof-20260521T132154Z
```

Slack observed behavior:
- The driver bot posted two real Slack messages into the configured channel asking the SUT bot to reply with unique `SLACK_QA_ECHO_*` markers.
- The lane failed with `timed out after 45000ms waiting for Slack message; retried 1x` and `slack-qa-observed-messages.json` was empty.

The Slack attempt did not produce a successful reply, so it is recorded as a transport-path attempt rather than the passing provider proof. The passing live Gemini proof above exercises the issue-specific failing provider path directly.

## Tests
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm test extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts -- -t "cachedContent|guarded fetch transport"`
- `git diff --check`
- IDE lints for `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` and `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`: no diagnostics

## Out Of Scope
- Changing Gemini cached-content creation, refresh, or TTL behavior.
- Changing Slack/Socket Mode handling or two-bot QA harness behavior.
- Adding compatibility fallbacks that send dynamic tools or system prompts alongside `cachedContent`.

## Transparency
AI-assisted change; locally reviewed and validated with focused tests plus live Gemini provider proof.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: neeravmakwana
